body:
## Summary

Fixes #106.

- Replaces time-based Google Docs browser-model readiness with quiescence detection in both CLIs.
- Uses a non-empty fingerprint of chunk count plus serialized chunk payload bytes, with a configurable stability window and hard timeout.
- Adds JS diagnostics (`modelPollCount`, `modelStableForMs`, payload bytes) and Rust log fields (`poll_count`, `stable_for_ms`, `chunk_payload_bytes`).
- Documents tuning via `WEB_CAPTURE_GDOCS_STABILITY_MS` and `WEB_CAPTURE_GDOCS_MAX_WAIT_MS`.
- Preserves issue data, CI logs, related issue/PR metadata, code search, online sources, and local verification logs under `docs/case-studies/issue-106/`.

## Reproduction and Regression Coverage

The JS unit regression uses a fake browser model stream that returns one chunk first and the complete two-chunk model only after later polls. Before the fix it failed with only `First paragraph`; after the fix it waits and includes `Second paragraph`.

Rust coverage adds private unit tests for the quiescence tracker and verifies that payload-size changes are part of the fingerprint, so same-count chunk growth resets stability.

## Validation

- `npm test -- --runTestsByPath tests/unit/gdocs.test.js tests/integration/gdocs-public-doc.test.js`
- `npm run check`
- `npx playwright install chromium`
- `npm test -- --testPathIgnorePatterns="docker.test.js"`
- `cargo fmt --all -- --check`
- `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test --all-features`

Local logs are committed in `docs/case-studies/issue-106/`.

## Release Notes

- JS: patch changeset added.
- Rust: bumped to `0.3.9` and updated `rust/CHANGELOG.md`.
title: Fix Google Docs browser-model chunk quiescence
